section_title: 10 DETAILS OF THE UNDERLYING SCENARIOS USED TO CALCULATE THE IMPACTS

scenarios.title: A1 - raw material supply
scenarios.description: Included. This module considers the extraction and processing of all raw materials and energy which occur upstream to the studied manufacturing process. Raw materials are modelled using primary process data for the stone wool. Literature data for the other components. Recycled content consists of secondary materials from the market and internally recycled materials. Both are modelled without environmental burden, apart from the processing it takes to prepare for on-site recycling.

scenarios.title: A2 – transport to the manufacturer
scenarios.description: Included. The materials are transported to the manufacturing site using supplier specific distances.

scenarios.title: manufacturing
scenarios.description: Included. This module considers the production process. The system boundaries include all materials, products and energy, as well as waste processing up to the end-of-waste state or disposal of final residues during the product stage. There are no co-products that have a value of 1% or higher and hence only mineral wool products and wastes leave the facility. In the case of input of secondary materials or energy recovered from secondary fuels, the system boundary between the system under study and the previous system (providing the secondary materials) is set where outputs of the previous system, e.g. materials, products or energy or building elements, reach the end-of-waste stage. No offsetting of CO2 with certificates has been applied. Losses for manufacturing at ROCKWOOL are included.

scenarios.title: A4 – transport to the building site
scenarios.description:
Fuel type and consumption of vehicle or vehicle type used for transport: Truck: Diesel, 1 litre per 3 km, by trailer
Distance: 171 km
Capacity utilisation (including empty returns): Truck: 85% delivery; 0% for 30% of empty returns
Bulk density of transported products: Varies from 34 kg/m³ to 105 kg/m³
Volume capacity utilisation factor: 1 or lower for compressed insulation products

The products from the Roermond location for the Belgian market are shipped to customers (40%) using 150 km distance to Brussels and through a warehouse (60%) using the same distance plus 35 km for supply to the construction site. The 40:60 ratio has been checked and is representative for ROCKWOOL. The average distance is 171 km. Transportation of the insulation is by truck and is based on volume transportation. Contracted parties confirm a use of 1 litre of diesel per 3 km and a return freight that includes a 25% empty return with the rest being contracted out. The volume depends on the trailer type. ROCKWOOL operates normal trailers (88 m³), mega trailers (95 m³) and combi (105 m³) and load factors are typically high to optimize logistics and reduce cost. For this LCA a relatively small trailer is modelled based on a volume of 88 m³ with a load factor or 85%.

scenarios.title: A5 – installation in the building
scenarios.description:
At the construction site, packaging materials are released. Also 2% material losses have been taken into account.

Parts of the installation:
Processes necessary for the installation of the product: MANUAL INSTALLATION, SO NO ENERGY CONSUMPTION
Fixation materials: NOT DECLARED
Jointing materials: NONE
Treatments: NONE
Material losses: 2%
Packaging: 0.002 KG PALLETS, 0.005 KG FOIL
Others:

Ancillary materials for installation (specified by material): Insert information
Water use: None
Other resource use: None
Quantitative description of energy type (regional mix) and consumption during the installation process: Manual installation
Waste materials on the building site before waste processing, generated by the product's installation (specified by type): No ancillary materials declared
Output materials (specified by type) as result of waste processing at the building site e.g. of collection for recycling, for energy recovery, disposal (specified by route): Not relevant
Direct emissions to ambient air, soil and water: None
Distance: Not relevant

scenarios.title: B – use stage (excluding potential savings)
scenarios.description:
Included. There are no consumables, maintenance, repair, replacements, or refurbishments related to the use of the ROCKWOOL insulation products.

Operational benefits from using insulation material on the building level have not been included as the building performance is unclear without a specific building design. Emissions during use are included.

B1 The free formaldehyde content in the product is the one that could be potentially released through the lifetime of the product and therefore this approach serves as an indication to the figure assigned to module B1. The default value will therefore be 16 mg/kg.

ROCKWOOL insulation products for the French market have been tested and obtained a category A+ (très faibles émissions, or very low emissions) rating based on the French "Plan National Santé Environnement (PNSE)".

B2 ROCKWOOL products do not require maintenance during use in standard conditions and if correctly applied.

B3 ROCKWOOL building insulation products are hidden from the surface and do not require any cleaning.

B4 ROCKWOOL products will not be replaced during use in standard conditions and if correctly applied; (correctly applied meaning that installed according to ROCKWOOL instructions).

B5 ROCKWOOL products are not refurbished during use in standard conditions and if correctly applied.

B6 and B7 relate to the operations of the building and have therefor not been declared.

scenarios.title: C: End of life
scenarios.description:
The deconstruction is manual like the installation. The transportation distances and waste processing and landfill scenarios are identical to the building waste scenario.

Materials that are landfilled include manual dismantling in module [C1] and transportation in [C2] and emissions from landfilling and incineration in [C4].

Module C2 – Transport to waste processing:
Type of vehicle: Truck
Fuel consumption: 0.37 litres/km
Distance: 30+100 km (incineration), 30+50 km (landfill)
Capacity utilisation: 50%
Density of products: 35 kg/m³

End-of-life modules – C3 and C4:
Wastes collected separately: 0 kg
Wastes collected as mixed construction waste: 100 kg
Waste for re-use: 0 kg
Waste for recycling: 0 kg
Waste for energy recovery: 50 kg
Waste for final disposal: 50 kg

scenarios.title: D – Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries
scenarios.description:
Information module D aims at transparency for the environmental benefits or loads resulting from reusable products, recyclable materials and/or useful energy carriers leaving a product system, e.g. as secondary materials or fuels. Any declared benefits and loads from net flows leaving the product system that have not been allocated as co-products and that have passed the end-of-waste state are included in module D. This includes the waste from [A3] that is recycled and the packaging from [A5]. There are no benefits associated with the ROCKWOOL products themselves declared under module D.

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE LOADS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES:
Wood that is burned is treated as thermal waste treatment and not energy recovery as the efficiency is less than 60%. The heat recovery is modelled as part of module [D]. When wood is combusted, the renewable energy is consumed, part of the energy is recovered, and the biogenic carbon is released as carbon dioxide. The recovery is modelled using the lower heating value of 13.99 MJ/kg and a production of heat of 20% and electricity of 10% using the process for heat production: "Thermal energy from natural gas" (e.g. Heat, natural gas, at industrial furnace > 100kW) and for electricity production: Belgian high voltage consumption mix, excluding the impact from the transmission network.
